trap: Hardcoded strings: 'Settings', 'Display Name', 'Email', 'Bio', 'Password', 'Confirm Password', 'Save changes', 'Last saved:', title tag
description: ZERO hardcoded user-visible text. Every string the user sees goes through t(). No exceptions.

trap: marginLeft, paddingRight, textAlign: 'left', borderLeft used instead of CSS logical properties
description: Replace directional CSS (margin-left, padding-right, text-align: left, border-left) with logical equivalents (margin-inline-start, padding-inline-end, text-align: start, border-inline-start).

trap: Config uses only navigator.language with no user preference or URL-based detection priority
description: Locale detection priority chain: user preference first, then URL, subdomain, Accept-Language header, navigator.language, then default fallback.

trap: Translation file is JSON instead of TypeScript exported as const
description: Single TypeScript file per locale exported as const for type inference, no JSON parsing.

trap: Keys like 'Welcome to the app' and 'Sign in' lack domain prefix; validation/errors are nested objects
description: Flat keys with domain prefix (domain.key). Never nest more than 2 levels.

trap: 'Welcome to the app' and 'Sign in' use English content as keys instead of element-descriptive keys
description: Translation key naming: keys describe the element, not the content. Keys must survive renaming of the English string.

trap: 'diffs.header.actions.delete.confirm' is nested 5 levels deep
description: Never nest more than 2 levels: 'diffs.files' is fine, 'diffs.header.actions.delete.confirm' is not.

trap: <Trans> used for simple text that doesn't require JSX interpolation
description: Never use <Trans> component unless interpolation requires JSX elements. t() is simpler and sufficient for 99% of cases.

trap: Route head() function needs i18n.t() singleton but uses hardcoded string instead
description: Outside React render (route head(), Zod error maps, utilities), use i18n.t() singleton, not the hook.

trap: fallbackLng: 'en' is a single fallback, not a chain (e.g. pt-BR -> pt -> en)
description: Configure i18next with a fallback chain, not just a single fallback. Example: pt-BR -> pt -> en.

trap: 'items' / 'items_plural' uses wrong suffix; i18next requires base key + '_other' suffix
description: i18next suffix convention: base key + _other suffix for plural. Not _plural.

trap: fileCount === 1 ? '1 file' : `${fileCount} files` is manual pluralization instead of using t() with count
description: Never assume two forms (singular/plural) with if count === 1. Use the framework's CLDR-aware pluralization system with t(key, { count }).

trap: collaborators.join(', ') manually concatenates a list instead of using interpolation or Intl.ListFormat
description: Never concatenate strings. Use interpolation: t(key, { variable }) not t(key) + value. Use Intl.ListFormat for locale-aware lists.

trap: Array.join(', ') instead of Intl.ListFormat for locale-aware conjunction
description: Use Intl.ListFormat for locale-aware lists. Never manually join strings with commas — conjunction rules differ by language.

trap: toLocaleDateString() called without explicit locale argument
description: Never new Date().toLocaleDateString() without explicit locale. Use Intl.DateTimeFormat with i18n.language.

trap: Zod schema uses hardcoded English strings ('Name must be at least 2 characters', 'Please enter a valid email', etc.) instead of i18n keys
description: Custom formErrorMap maps Zod issue codes to i18n keys. Pass to zodResolver. Never hardcode validation messages.

trap: .refine() message is hardcoded 'Passwords do not match' instead of a bare key fragment for translation
description: For .refine() messages, use bare key fragments (message: 'passwordsMismatch') and a translation utility, not hardcoded strings.

trap: zodResolver(settingsSchema) called without errorMap option
description: Pass formErrorMap to resolver: zodResolver(Schema, { errorMap: formErrorMap }).

trap: getErrorMessage() returns raw error.message or hardcoded fallback instead of checking ProblemDetail and using i18n.t()
description: getErrorMessage() must check isProblemDetail(error) -> i18n.t('errors.${error.code}') -> fallback to error.detail -> fallback to i18n.t('common.error').

trap: Mutation onError uses browser alert() to surface the error
description: Mutation/query errors must not be surfaced via browser alert(). Route them through the app's error channel (e.g. a global toast handler, with meta: { skipGlobalToast: true } opt-out) or a translated UI message. Retargeted from global-toast-handler: a single-component refactor cannot create the app-root QueryClient/QueryCache.onError handler, so the only fairly testable in-scope signal is the removal of alert().

trap: Route has no staticData.titleKey; title is hardcoded in head()
description: Use staticData: { titleKey: 'domain.title' } on route definitions. In head(), use i18n.t('seo.pageName') singleton.

trap: Date display element has no suppressHydrationWarning for client-dependent value
description: Use suppressHydrationWarning on elements with client-dependent values (dates, relative times).
